summary: O'Neil announces her Slate essay 'The Ethical Data Scientist' which argues for bringing better ethics to data science practice. She highlights that ethics education should be integrated into data science training programs. She points to Berkeley's School of Information as a model, noting their undergraduate and graduate courses on data science ethics. The post serves as both promotion for her longer piece and a call for readers to share other ethics education initiatives in the field.
key_points: Data scientists need formal ethics education as part of their training
Berkeley's School of Information offers model courses on data science ethics at both undergraduate and graduate levels
The field needs more visibility into existing ethics education efforts
Ethics in data science is an emerging but underdeveloped area of focus
money_quotes: One thing I call for in the essay is the teaching of ethics to aspiring data scientists
key_insight: Ethics education must become a standard component of data science training, not an afterthought.
